extraction.one_line_summary: THOMAS Geraint won Stage 12 atop Alpe d'Huez with a devastating acceleration out of the final corner, dropping DUMOULIN and BARDET in the last 200m while wearing yellow.
tactical_patterns: THOMAS accelerated out of final corner at 25-30mph on 6-7% gradient, proving track background translates to corner exit power.
BERNAL Egan at 21 controlled Sky train solo on Alpe d'Huez, shutting down NIBALI/QUINTANA attacks before veterans cracked.
KRUIJSWIJK Stefan's 6+ minute breakaway lead evaporated on Alpe d'Huez, revealing limits of solo long-range attacks on iconic climbs.
Sky maintained dual leadership with FROOME despite THOMAS winning twice in yellow, suggesting team genuinely uncertain about hierarchy.

rider_signals.THOMAS Geraint: positive: Expressing surprise at own victory suggests genuine form breakthrough beyond pre-race expectations, not controlled performance
rider_signals.BERNAL Egan: positive: 21-year-old controlled race solo for Sky on Alpe d'Huez, showing Grand Tour maturity years ahead of schedule
rider_signals.NIBALI Vincenzo: negative: Had winning position but mechanical/crash cost stage, suggesting bad luck not form deficit
rider_signals.FROOME Chris: neutral: Lost time to teammate again but still treated as co-leader, indicating Sky sees three-week durability concerns with Thomas
rider_signals.LATOUR Pierre: negative: Attacking Colombiere nearly cost white jersey, showing poor race management for GC placement
rider_signals.KRUIJSWIJK Steven: negative: Six-minute solo advantage collapsed completely on Alpe d'Huez, revealing he lacks diesel to sustain long breakaways
